task_completion.description: Open the inventory spreadsheet located at /home/ga/Documents/inventory.xlsx
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) is open displaying the inventory.xlsx file, showing columns like Quantity, Reorder Level, and Unit Price.

task_completion.description: Highlight cells in the Quantity column in red when the value is less than the Reorder Level
task_completion.visual_evidence: The conditional formatting dialog shows a rule comparing the Quantity column to the Reorder Level column with a red style applied, OR the spreadsheet shows cells in the Quantity column with a red background/text specifically where the number is lower than the corresponding Reorder Level.

task_completion.description: Highlight cells in the Unit Price column in green when the price is greater than 100
task_completion.visual_evidence: The conditional formatting dialog shows a rule for the Unit Price column with the condition '> 100' and a green style, OR the spreadsheet shows cells in the Unit Price column with a green background/text where the value exceeds 100.

task_completion.description: Apply data bars to the Quantity column
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Quantity column displays horizontal data bars within the cells, visually representing the relative size of the quantity values.

task_completion.description: Apply a green-to-red color scale to the Unit Price column
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Unit Price column displays a color gradient (green to red) across its cells based on the numerical values.

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the 'Save' icon or uses the File > Save menu, and there is no 'unsaved changes' indicator (like an asterisk next to the filename in the title bar) after the formatting is applied.

integrity.description: The agent used a spreadsheet GUI application to apply the formatting
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a spreadsheet application's interface, menus, and conditional formatting dialog boxes, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The formatting is applied using built-in conditional formatting features, not manual coloring
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Conditional Formatting' dialog box is visible in screenshots showing the rules being configured, rather than the agent manually selecting cells and changing their background color one by one.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script or CLI tool to bypass GUI interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows do not show scripts (e.g., using openpyxl or pandas) being written or executed to modify inventory.xlsx directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains exactly 30 items derived from Montgomery County MD Warehouse & Retail Sales. The expected conditional formatting rules correspond to 'cellIs' (for the less than/greater than comparisons), 'dataBar' (for the quantity bars), and 'colorScale' (for the price gradient). Verify that the visual effects in the spreadsheet match these specific rule types.
